# UC-14.1.10 · SNMP Trap Storm Detection

## Description

Trap floods overwhelm collectors and obscure real faults; rapid detection enables rate limiting and upstream device triage.

## Value

Trap floods overwhelm collectors and obscure real faults; rapid detection enables rate limiting and upstream device triage.

## Implementation

Baseline traps/min per agent IP. Alert when rate exceeds 5× baseline or absolute threshold. Correlate with link flaps or misconfigured threshold on managed device.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP trap receiver, `snmptrapd` → syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype="snmp:trap"` or `snmptrapd:syslog`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Baseline traps/min per agent IP. Alert when rate exceeds 5× baseline or absolute threshold. Correlate with link flaps or misconfigured threshold on managed device.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype IN ("snmp:trap","snmptrapd:syslog")
| timechart span=1m count as trap_rate by device_ip
| where trap_rate > 500
```

Understanding this SPL

**SNMP Trap Storm Detection** — Trap floods overwhelm collectors and obscure real faults; rapid detection enables rate limiting and upstream device triage.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype="snmp:trap"` or `snmptrapd:syslog`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP trap receiver, `snmptrapd` → syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1m** buckets with a separate series **by device_ip** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where trap_rate > 500` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search in a known window. Spot-check a few events against the BMS console (for example Niagara, Tridium, or Honeywell) and confirm zones, values, and alarms line up. Verify field names and that the right roles can search the index.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (trap rate by device), Single value (peak traps/min), Table (top storm sources).

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype IN ("snmp:trap","snmptrapd:syslog")
| timechart span=1m count as trap_rate by device_ip
| where trap_rate > 500
```

## Visualization

Line chart (trap rate by device), Single value (peak traps/min), Table (top storm sources).
